response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and systems software engineer with ~14 years of professional history, strong low-latency and embedded systems exposure, and recent AI-agent tooling work. However, the Principal Performance Architect role demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, GPU/AI accelerator performance analysis, SIMD/MIMD architectures, and hardware-software co-design for deep learning workloads — none of which appear anywhere in the resume. His technical depth is real but oriented entirely toward web infrastructure, developer tooling, and application-layer performance, placing him in a fundamentally different engineering domain from what this role requires.
strengths: ~14 years of continuous engineering experience including low-latency systems work (async I/O, high-throughput microservices at Dunnhumby) demonstrates performance-conscious engineering mindset
Hands-on embedded and hardware-adjacent work (robotic arms at Altom, wave field synthesis audio system for thesis) shows some comfort beyond pure software abstraction layers
Recent AI-agent infrastructure contribution at re:cinq (MCP frameworks, agentic workflows) indicates exposure to the AI ecosystem, even if at the tooling/application layer
concerns: Zero demonstrable experience with SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, or hardware performance simulation — the core technical requirements of the role
No background in deep learning training or inference optimization, SIMD/MIMD parallelism, or AI accelerator workloads; the skill set is entirely software-stack oriented

Education is a dual Bachelor's (CS + Fine Arts) with no graduate degree; while the role accepts equivalent experience, the candidate's experience does not substitute for the missing hardware architecture specialization

key_factors.factor: No deep learning or GPU workload expertise
Strong software engineering tenure with low-latency exposure
